steps.title: Identify the angle relationship
steps.content: From the figure, the angle marked $x^{\circ}$ and the angle marked $145^{\circ}$ are formed by the transversal $k$ cutting the parallel lines $m$ and $n$, in positions that make them vertical angles (or corresponding angles between parallel lines — both relationships force equality).

steps.title: Apply the theorem and check each choice
steps.content:
Vertical angles are congruent, and corresponding angles formed by a transversal cutting parallel lines are also congruent. Either way, $x = 145$.
  • A) $x < 145$: false, since $x = 145$. Eliminate.
  • B) $x > 145$: false, since $x = 145$. Eliminate.
  • C) $x = 145$: matches the vertical/corresponding angle relationship. Match.
  • D) Cannot be determined: false — the parallel-line and transversal setup pins $x$ exactly. Eliminate.
That matches choice C — C is correct.
